testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
target_audience: Professionals working in data centers, on-premises, and hybrid environments
recommended_experience: CompTIA A+ certified or equivalent knowledge, with two years of hands-on experience in a server environment

prerequisites.description: CompTIA A+ certified or equivalent knowledge
Two years of hands-on experience in a server environment

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Install physical hardware
sub_objectives: racking, cabling, power, and cooling management

objectives.title: Deploy and manage storage
sub_objectives: RAID levels, shared storage, and capacity planning

objectives.title: Perform hardware maintenance
sub_objectives: out-of-band management, firmware upgrades, and hot-swappable components

objectives.title: Install server operating systems
sub_objectives: partition types, file systems, and installation methods

objectives.title: Configure network services
sub_objectives: IP addressing, DNS, DHCP, and VLANs

objectives.title: Manage server functions
sub_objectives: roles, monitoring, data migration, and performance metrics

objectives.title: High availability
sub_objectives: clustering, load balancing, and failover processes

objectives.title: Virtualization
sub_objectives: host vs. guest, resource allocation, and cloud models

objectives.title: Scripting basics
sub_objectives: loops, variables, and common server tasks

objectives.title: Asset management
sub_objectives: documentation, lifecycle management, and secure storage

objectives.title: Data security
sub_objectives: encryption, retention policies, and lifecycle management

objectives.title: Physical security
sub_objectives: access controls, environmental controls, and biometric systems

objectives.title: Identity and access management
sub_objectives: user accounts, MFA, and permissions

objectives.title: Mitigation strategies
sub_objectives: malware prevention, DLP, and SIEM

objectives.title: Server hardening
sub_objectives: OS updates, disabling unused services, and host security

objectives.title: Decommissioning
sub_objectives: media destruction, recycling, and asset management

objectives.title: Troubleshoot hardware
sub_objectives: power issues, storage failures, and connectivity problems

objectives.title: Troubleshoot software
sub_objectives: OS errors, application issues, and patching failures

objectives.title: Troubleshoot network
sub_objectives: latency, misconfigurations, and security breaches

objectives.title: Disaster recovery
sub_objectives: backup strategies, recovery testing, and failover validation

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Learn
official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
